title: RedVox: Safety and Fairness Gaps in Speech Models Across Languages
abstract: arXiv:2606.26968v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Speech-capable models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications across languages. Yet their safety and fairness beyond English settings and under naturalistic conditions remain understudied. We survey safety reporting practices across state-of-the-art speech model releases, finding that only 8% document any multilingual analysis. To address this gap, we introduce RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for audio and speech built on real voices, covering unsafe and unfair stereotypical requests across five languages (English, French, Italian, Spanish, and German). Evaluating eight state-of-the-art models, we find that vulnerabilities persist even under non-adversarial conditions, worsen in non-English languages, and are amplified when the request comes from a spoken input. Finally, by surveying the participants who contributed to RedVox, we document the unique personal and privacy challenges of collecting speech da

Title: RedVox: Safety and Fairness Gaps in Speech Models Across Languages
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26968v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Speech-capable models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications across languages. Yet their safety and fairness beyond English settings and under naturalistic conditions remain understudied. We survey safety reporting practices across state-of-the-art speech model releases, finding that only 8% document any multilingual analysis. To address this gap, we introduce RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for audio and speech built on real voices, covering unsafe and unfair stereotypical requests across five languages (English, French, Italian, Spanish, and German). Evaluating eight state-of-the-art models, we find that vulnerabilities persist even under non-adversarial conditions, worsen in non-English languages, and are amplified when the request comes from a spoken input. Finally, by surveying the participants who contributed to RedVox, we document the unique personal and privacy challenges of collecting speech da
translated_title: RedVox：跨语言语音模型中的安全性与公平性差距
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26968v1 公告类型：新论文 摘要：具备语音能力的模型正越来越多地被部署在跨语言的实际应用中。然而，它们在英语环境之外以及在自然条件下的安全性和公平性研究仍然不足。我们调查了最先进的语音模型发布中的安全性报告实践，发现只有8%的模型记录了任何多语言分析。为了弥补这一差距，我们引入了RedVox，这是一个基于真实语音的多语言音频与语音安全性和公平性基准测试，覆盖了五种语言（英语、法语、意大利语、西班牙语和德语）中不安全和不公平的刻板印象请求。通过对八个最先进模型的评估，我们发现即使在非对抗性条件下，漏洞依然存在，在非英语语言中更为严重，并且当请求来自语音输入时，问题会被放大。最后，通过对参与RedVox数据收集的参与者进行调查，我们记录了收集语音数据所特有的个人和隐私挑战。
tldr: 本研究通过构建多语言语音安全性与公平性基准RedVox，揭示了当前语音模型在非英语环境下安全漏洞显著加剧、且语音输入比文本输入更易触发不公平刻板印象的问题。
en_tdlr: This study introduces RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for speech, revealing that vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art speech models are significantly worse in non-English languages and amplified by spoken input.
